headline: Tabung Haji Investment Losses Resurface With RM1.4B Write-Down
context: Lembaga Tabung Haji disclosed a RM1.4 billion write-down on legacy property and equity investments in its 2025 annual report, reviving concerns about the fund's management of 9.3 million depositors' hajj savings.

cards.big: RM1.4 billion written off — Tabung Haji's 9.3 million depositors lose value on their hajj savings again
cards.sub: Legacy investments in TRX land, FGV shares, and Indonesian plantations account for 78% of the write-down

cards.big: Tabung Haji's net asset value at RM78 billion still shows RM3.2 billion shortfall against depositor liabilities
cards.sub: Government injected RM17.8 billion in 2018-2020 to fill previous shortfall — new losses erode that rescue

cards.big: 9.3 million depositors — majority B40 and M40 Bumiputera — treat TH as a savings-plus-hajj vehicle
cards.sub: Average deposit of RM8,400 per account means losses disproportionately hurt low-wealth households

cards.big: TH board approved related-party transactions worth RM2.8 billion between 2015-2020 with insufficient disclosure
cards.sub: Post-reform board still includes 4 political appointees versus Shariah committee's recommendation of full independence

cards.big: Tabung Haji holds the sacred savings of 9.3 million Muslims — managing it like a political slush fund is a betrayal of trust
Should Tabung Haji be regulated by BNM as a deposit-taking institution with full banking standards?
